diagnosis: The level is a 200+ tile wide horizontal corridor with a repeating stair-step platform pattern and identical enemy placements on each step. The player spent ~32 seconds idle at spawn, then traversed the entire level in ~100 seconds with only one combat death at x=1714 (early section). The trace shows repeated backtracking and oscillation on flat stretches (e.g., x=3000-3300, x=6700-6900, x=8600-8900) where the player paced on the same elevation. Enemies (types 1,2,3,L) are placed on the same horizontal plane as the player's path with no vertical offset, making them trivial to bypass or shoot from below. The player completed the level but rated it 1/5, explicitly calling out repetition and simple enemies.

lessons.lesson: Repeating identical platform-enemy clusters at uniform horizontal intervals creates a predictable rhythm that players optimize away, eliminating engagement even when individual jumps or enemies are functional in isolation.
conditions: platform pattern repeats at fixed spacing
enemy types and positions repeat per cluster
no elevation change or structural breaks between clusters
lessons.outcome: player traverses quickly with minimal input variation, backtracks on flat sections, rates level 1/5 for repetition
